summary: Clerk-gated /admin/weekly editor with server-action MDX append
body:
Mirror of the ce-plan pattern: dynamic-imported ClerkAuthGate (ssr:false),
owner-only auth via sessionClaims email + currentUser() fallback, ISO-week
math to locate/create the current week's MDX, gray-matter parse+append+
stringify, and revalidatePath on /, /weekly, and /weekly/<slug>.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: commits-and-history sidebar with weekly deep-links
body:
Adds CommitsSidebar (server component, <details> accordion) grouped by
conventional-commit type. feat/fix expand by default; all others collapsed.
Each row shows date, hash linked to GitHub, type-scope pill, summary, and
an optional →/weekly/<slug> link when the commit date falls in a published
weekly log's window. Wires into the /changelog 2-col layout as a sticky
desktop aside (top-20 / max-h calc).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: parse CHANGELOG.md, link bidirectionally with weekly logs
body:
- lib/changelog-md.ts: hand-rolled regex parser for Keep-a-Changelog
  1.1.0 format. Exports typed ChangelogRelease[] with version, date, id,
  and sections map. Cached in module scope; degrades gracefully on
  parse errors. findReleaseInWeek() matches a release date to a week range.

- app/changelog/page.tsx: new /changelog route rendering parsed CHANGELOG
  in a 2-column layout (2/3 left, 1/3 right). Left column shows all
  releases with color-coded section badges (Added=teal, Changed=cyan,
  Fixed=green). Right column placeholder aside for CommitsSidebar (U9).

- app/weekly/[slug]/page.tsx: imports findReleaseInWeek(); renders a
  Released in CHANGELOG: vX.Y.Z green badge near the page title when
  the week contains a release. Renders nothing when no release matches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: merge Now + This Week into one section with 4 rails
body:
Replace CurrentUpdates component and inline "Now" eyebrow with a single
NowSection server component that stacks 4 anchor-linked rails: Milestones
(top 3 featured timeline entries), Writing (top 3 posts), Media (top 3
Medium articles), Projects (top 3 with /projects/<slug> deep-links).
Each rail has a sub-eyebrow label and a "View all →" link to the
corresponding route. In-section anchor nav at top for keyboard/skip access.

Deletes CurrentUpdates.tsx whose writing + media reach is now covered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: paginated rolling log of weekly updates
body:
Replace single-card "This Week" block with RollingLog server component
that paginates all weekly logs 5 per page via ?page=N URL param. Each
card shows ISO-week badge, date range, title, item count, top 3 tags,
and a read-the-log link. Pagination controls only render when >1 page
exists; out-of-range pages show an empty state with back link.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- supercritical-fluids-paper (physics)
- aggregation-theory (finance)
- claude-code-as-an-os (software)
- why-typescript-strict (software)

Added 308 redirects from old /knowledge/<domain>/<slug> URLs to /writing/<slug>.
Added originalDomain field to moved article frontmatter for origin tracking.
Removed empty physics and software knowledge domains from KNOWLEDGE_DOMAINS.
Finance domain kept (still has index and special thesis/trade sections).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: clickable + fzf tag index on /weekly
body:
- Collect all items across all weekly logs via getAllWeeklyLogs +
  getAllItems to build a global tag-frequency map (top 30 tags).
- app/weekly/page.tsx now accepts searchParams.tag; when set, filters
  the weekly card grid to logs that contain at least one item tagged
  with that value. Shows an active-tag pill + "× clear filter" link.
- New components/weekly/TagFzf.tsx (client component): controlled input
  that filters the visible badge cloud via String.includes; pressing
  Enter navigates to /weekly?tag=<first-match>. No new dependencies
  (cmdk already present; simple filter is more appropriate for inline
  badge cloud than command-palette UX).
- Active tag highlighted in teal; first-match on search highlighted in
  cyan. Empty-match state shows a descriptive message.

Remove /experience from the codebase, navigation links, sitemap, CommandPalette (via NAV_LINKS removal), and architecture diagram. Add a 308 permanent redirect from /experience to /about#career in next.config.js to preserve inbound links. The about page contains all career information under the Career section.

summary: per-project detail pages mirroring /writing/[slug] pattern
body:
- New dynamic route /projects/<slug> with generateStaticParams over PROJECTS + WORK_TOOLS, generateMetadata for OG/canonical, breadcrumb JSON-LD.
- Slug derivation via lib/projects.ts: kebab-case of name, parens-stripped; collisions between PROJECTS/WORK_TOOLS get a -tool suffix.
- Page renders full description, highlights, run-it commands, tech badges, GitHub CTA. Same density and chrome as /writing/<slug>.
- ProjectCard footer now exposes both "Quick look" (modal) and "Read more →" (page) so the canonical URL is one click away. Middle-click + cmd-click work as expected.
- Sitemap picks up every project URL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: flat item grid + cached git changelog + searchable tag filters
body:
- Drop the 6-rail bucket UX. Replace with one flat card grid (3-col lg, 2-col md, 1 mobile). Each card shows title, source, kind badge, notes preview; click opens the existing description modal (anchor section -> notes -> fallback).
- Sticky filter bar above the grid: search (matches title/source/notes/tags), kind facet chips (Video/Podcast/Article/Repo/Meeting/Note...), and frequency-ranked tag pills (top 8 + "+N more"). All three filters serialize into ?q=&tag=&kind= for shareable URLs (lib/url-state.ts).
- New lib/weekly-types.ts (client-safe) split out from lib/weekly.ts (server-only) so the grid can import types without dragging fs into the client bundle. Same split for lib/git-commit.ts vs lib/git-changelog.ts.
- Per-item tag derivation: log-level tags propagate to every item; item-level tags add to the pool; kind + source auto-tag too.
- Auto-promote pure-string learned items to {text, kind:'note'} so every entry has a stable shape.
- Cached git changelog under the grid: scripts/sync-git-changelog.mjs reads `git log --no-merges -n 200`, parses conventional-commit type/scope/multi-scope tolerantly, writes content/_generated/git-changelog.json. Wired as predev + prebuild in package.json.
- New <GitChangelog> component (components/sections/GitChangelog.tsx) renders commits grouped by day, with type/scope tag pills, search, "this week / all time" scope toggle, and click-to-expand commit body.
- Hide the long top-level tag wall on /weekly/[slug] and /weekly. Tags now live as filters in the grid (per the user's ask).
- Stagger entry animation on grid cards (30ms cap at 12 cards), motion-reduce safe.
- Modal unchanged (max-w-3xl, bg-surface, body-portaled, Emil-framework animation).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: bigger surface (max-w-3xl), bg-surface for token consistency
body:
- Bumped from max-w-lg (32rem) to max-w-3xl (48rem); max-h 78vh -> 82vh.
- bg-background -> bg-surface so the modal uses the elevated-card token
  shared by the rest of the design system.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: centered, focused, animated per Emil's framework
body:
- Smaller, square-ish footprint: max-w-lg / max-h-78vh, centered via flex.
- Body-portal (createPortal) escapes ancestor transform that was scoping
  position:fixed away from the viewport.
- Entry: scale(0.96) + translateY(8px) + opacity(0) -> scale(1) at 220ms
  with cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1). Exit at 160ms (asymmetric).
- Backdrop: bg-black/80 + backdrop-blur-md, opacity transition.
- transform-origin: center (modal exception per Emil).
- Buttons: active:scale-[0.92] feedback, transition specifies exact props.
- Body: scroll-smooth + overscroll-behavior contain.
- prefers-reduced-motion: opacity-only transition.
- Single source of truth for ESC handler (child manages); parent keeps
  body scroll lock.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

- New /ai-hardware-stack route wraps public/ai-hardware-stack.html in a
  same-origin iframe under the site chrome; surfaced as a Featured deck card
  on /knowledge/ai; sitemap entry added.

summary: contain long shell snippets on mobile
body:
The "How to use these skills" three-up grid had pre/code blocks with
unbreakable shell commands (curl, mkdir). Grid items default to
min-width: auto = min-content, which on mobile equals the longest
unwrappable token, so the cell expanded past the viewport and pushed
the whole page horizontally — observed 565px scrollWidth on a 390px
viewport.

Adding min-w-0 to the three grid children lets each cell shrink below
its content's natural minimum, allowing the inner pre's
overflow-x-auto to actually engage. /skills viewport probe goes from
hOverflow: true (175px excess) to clean.

Audited every route at iPhone width (390x844) — every other route
was already responsive-clean. No other fixes needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: rich rail items + home-page surfacing
body:
Weekly logs now render each rail item as a linked card with thumbnail
or source logo. Each entry can be a plain string (quick logging) or
{ text, href, image, source, kind } — YouTube URLs auto-derive
mqdefault thumbnails, common sources (GitHub/Substack/Latent Space/
Medium/X/arXiv/Spotify) auto-fetch SimpleIcons logos so authoring
stays terse.

Adds a "This week" section to the home page directly under the hero,
showing the latest weekly log's title, week range, six-category
counts grid, and tags. Routes the visitor straight into /weekly/<slug>
so the running log is one click off the landing page.

Updates 2026-W18 to use the new rich format (YouTube + Latent Space
podcast + a16z + GitHub repos with auto-resolved logos).

summary: weekly logs + highlights data bank + featured banners + structural rework

banner system. Restructures /writing to merge in /learnings as a tabbed view (Essays |
Learnings), drops the Second Brain rail from home and /writing, and demotes /knowledge
to secondary nav. Per-skill Benmore credit + per-skill install snippet on every detail
page; expanded LLM symlink/install commands on /skills (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex,
Continue/Aider/Cline, Web LLM). Benmore badge scoped to /skills only — removed from nav
and footer. /learnings stays as a 308 redirect to /writing.

2.3.0 → 2.4.0, architecture page gains a Skills-library diagram (now seven), release
note at docs/release-notes/2026-05-02-skills-library.md.

summary: aggressive social-graph linking for identity indexing
body:
Goal: every search engine + AI crawler immediately resolves all of Arkash's
profiles to one Person.

Surfaces added:
- lib/site.ts: add ORCID, PubMed, BU CS profile URLs to SITE.social
- app/layout.tsx <head>: 10 <link rel="me"> entries (LinkedIn, GitHub,
  X/Twitter, Substack, Medium, ORCID, Scholar, Harvard lab, BU profile,
  email) — IndieWeb identity verification + fediverse two-way handshake
- app/layout.tsx <head>: 10 <meta property="og:see_also"> tags pointing at
  every public profile — Google SGE / Bing Copilot ingest these
- Person JSON-LD sameAs: now 11 entries (added ORCID, PubMed, BU)
- New <SocialLinks/> component with grid + inline variants. Every link
  carries rel="me me-author noopener noreferrer" and a short subtitle.
- /  page: new "Connect — Find me everywhere" section with the grid
- /about page: new "Connect — Where to find me" section after the timeline
- Footer already wires the same set; humans.txt + llms.txt already credit.

Net: 22 rel=me links, 10 og:see_also, 11-entry sameAs, all on every page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: index 'Arkash Jain' as author across every crawler surface
body:
- llms.txt + llms-full.txt: prepend 'Maintained by Arkash Jain — arkashj.com'
- robots.txt: convert to static public/robots.txt with credit header comment
  block (replaces app/robots.ts so the file actually contains comments).
- humans.txt: new — full credits file at site root (TEAM, ROLE, SITE, THANKS).
- HTML <head>: add explicit <meta name=author/creator/designer/copyright/
  owner> + <link rel=author> + article:author OpenGraph property.
- <noscript> credit line in body root — visible in view-source on every page,
  indexed by every crawler regardless of JS state.
- WebSite JSON-LD: add author / publisher / creator / copyrightHolder /
  mainEntity all referencing the Person@id, plus copyrightYear.
- Footer: replace one-line tech credit with two-up layout — left side has
  '© <year> Arkash Jain — built & maintained by arkashj.com' with
  rel=author + itemProp=author; right side keeps tech credit + humans.txt
  link.

Net effect: every page emits Arkash's name to crawlers via meta tags,
JSON-LD, noscript fallback, and footer. Three top-level credit files
(robots.txt, humans.txt, llms.txt) all open with maintainer attribution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: track lib/ directory (was ignored by global gitignore)
body:
Global ~/.gitignore_global includes Python's lib/ rule, which silently
excluded our TypeScript lib/ from commits. Direct CLI deploys uploaded
local files; GitHub-triggered builds saw nothing and failed to resolve
@/lib/* imports. Local .gitignore now overrides with !lib/.

summary: /docs route renders project markdown · refresh README + CHANGELOG (#12)
body:
WHY NOT SWAGGER
This is a static content site, not an API. Swagger/Redoc document
OpenAPI specs — there is no API surface to document. The right analog
is rendering the project's own markdown docs as a polished in-site
reading experience.

NEW /docs ROUTE
- /docs hub page: cards grouped by category (Getting Started · Reference · Planning · Releases)
- /docs/[slug] detail: sticky sidebar + themed markdown rendering
- lib/docs.ts curates which markdown files appear; reads from filesystem at build time
- components/docs/MarkdownView.tsx — themed react-markdown with GFM tables
- components/docs/DocsSidebar.tsx
- Renders: README, HANDOFF, TODO, site-architecture, design spec, foundation plan, CHANGELOG
- Added /docs to NAV_LINKS

REFRESHED README
- Comprehensive shields.io badge row (license, version 2.2.0, status, CI, stack badges, tooling)
- Stack table updated to current Next 15 / React 19 / TS strict reality
- Pages enumeration shows all 14 routes
- Common commands cheatsheet
- Adding-content lookup table
- MDX embed examples
- ffmpeg recipes
- Custom domain instructions

REFRESHED CHANGELOG
- v2.2.0 entry — Metior visual identity, hero overhaul, theme system, /stack, /learnings, /media, /architecture diagrams, real links, MDX embeds, docs
- v2.1.0 entry — App Router + TypeScript migration, dynamic OG, more MDX
- v1.x history preserved
- Roadmap pointer to TODO.md

Build clean. /docs renders all 7 catalogued markdown files as static SSG pages.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: add TODO.md handoff for open work + cheatsheet

body:
The timeline now distinguishes major milestones from completed routine entries:
- Major (Published / Current / Live): primary teal node with ring shadow,
  brighter card border, larger title, gradient bottom rule
- Routine (Completed): subtle dark border, smaller title, no rule
- Reverse-chronological order (most-recent first)

Reads as a real timeline now, not a wall of identical cards.

body:
SectionHeader rebuilt:
- Uses Pill component (animated dot) for the eyebrow
- New optional italicAccent prop renders a warm-tan italic display under the title
- Larger title scale (3xl/4xl), tighter leading
- Bigger bottom border breathing room

Applied across:
- /about       → "Life Changelog. / In order. Real dates."
- /research    → "Four published papers. / One thesis."
- /experience  → "Where I've worked. / And what I shipped."
- /projects    → "Things I've built. / Research code, internal tooling, open source."
- /work        → "Tools & CLIs. / Used every day."
- /writing     → "Essays, notes, theses. / What I'm learning, in public."
- /knowledge   → "Second brain. / In public."
- /media       → "Podcasts, videos, articles. / The public footprint."
- /stack       → "What I use. / Updated when something earns it."
- /learnings   → "Lessons learned the hard way. / No platitudes."
- /architecture → "How this site is built. / Six diagrams."

Knowledge hub also got stagger animation on the domain card grid.

Visual treatment matches the Hero (Metior-Pro inspired) so every page has the
same restrained pill → bold title → italic-tan accent → muted description rhythm.

summary: Next.js 15 App Router + TypeScript strict migration (#5)
body:
FRAMEWORK
- Next.js 13 → 15.5
- React 18 → 19
- Pages Router → App Router (app/)
- Plain JS → TypeScript strict

NEW APP TREE
- app/layout.tsx (Person JSON-LD, Nav, Footer, OG metadataBase)
- app/page.tsx (homepage)
- app/about, /research, /experience, /projects, /work
- app/writing/page + [slug]/page (server components, generateStaticParams)
- app/knowledge/page + [domain]/page + [domain]/[slug]/page
- app/architecture/page + flows.ts
- app/sitemap.ts (native MetadataRoute, dynamic MDX routes)
- app/robots.ts (native MetadataRoute)
- app/not-found.tsx
- app/VC, /Volunteering — server-side redirects

MDX
- Switched MdxContent.tsx to next-mdx-remote/rsc (server component)
- lib/content.ts returns raw MDX source (RSC compiles at request time)

LIB
- lib/site.ts, lib/data.ts, lib/finance.ts, lib/content.ts → typed
- lib/structured-data.ts (Person, Article, ScholarlyArticle factories)
- lib/metadata.ts buildMetadata() factory typed against next/Metadata

COMPONENTS
- components/{ui, layout, sections, seo, architecture}/*.tsx — all typed
- Nav uses usePathname() hook with 'use client'
- Removed unused legacy mainPage/, battery/, volunteer/ component dirs

CONFIG
- tsconfig.json with strict mode + paths alias @/*
- types/css.d.ts for CSS side-effect imports
- tailwind.config.js content globs updated for app/ + ts/tsx
- .eslintrc.json disables no-html-link-for-pages (no pages/ dir)

DELETED
- pages/ (entire dir)
- styles/*.module.css
- styles/globals.css (moved to app/globals.css)

VERIFIED
- npm run build: 33 routes generated; static + SSG mix
- All routes return 200 in dev (curl + Playwright)
- Person JSON-LD on every page; ScholarlyArticle on /research
- MDX renders identically to v1 via RSC

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: 7 new MDX articles + sitemap rewrite for dynamic routes (#4)

CONTENT

- knowledge/finance/{aggregation-theory, index}.mdx
- knowledge/physics/{why-i-left-physics, supercritical-fluids-paper}.mdx
- knowledge/software/claude-code-as-an-os.mdx
- knowledge/math/optimizers.mdx

SITEMAP
- pages/sitemap.xml.js now enumerates every dynamic MDX route
- Per-file lastmod dates from frontmatter
- Knowledge domains added as their own routes

UI
- /knowledge hub shows real per-domain article counts (filesystem-driven)

Build SSGs 4 writing posts + 7 knowledge MDX files.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: MDX content pipeline + real essays (#3)
body:
CONTENT
- content/writing/{why-fde, sample-ai-hardware, distributed-checkpointing}.mdx
- content/knowledge/ai/{index, spatialdino-lessons}.mdx
- content/knowledge/distributed-systems/flink-checkpointing.mdx

PIPELINE
- lib/content.js: filesystem-backed loaders with frontmatter parsing
- components/MdxContent.js: themed MDX renderer (headings, lists, code, blockquotes)
- pages/writing/[slug].js → SSG via getStaticProps + getStaticPaths
- pages/knowledge/[domain]/index.js + [slug].js for nested MDX routes
- Homepage reads real posts (not the stubbed WRITING array)

DEPS
- gray-matter, next-mdx-remote

VERIFIED
- Build passes; 3 writing posts and 2 knowledge posts SSG'd
- All MDX routes return 200; visual check via Playwright

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

summary: Foundation + v2 site overhaul (#2)
body:
* feat: foundation quick wins — license, tooling, CI, /architecture page

- Apache 2.0 LICENSE, package.json → v1.0.0, project renamed arkashj-com
- README rewritten with shields.io badges, ffmpeg recipes
- CHANGELOG.md (Keep-a-Changelog) with v1.0.0 + v2.0.0 roadmap
- Dependabot config (security scanning, no auto-PRs)
- Prettier + .prettierignore + .editorconfig
- ESLint stricter rules with Prettier integration
- Husky + lint-staged pre-commit hook
- next.config.js: security headers, removed ignoreDuringBuilds
- vercel.json with caching + security headers
- GitHub Actions CI (lint + format:check + build)
- ASCII architecture flows in docs/architecture/site-architecture.md
- Public /architecture page rendering all 6 flows
- Fixed legacy unescaped apostrophes (lint errors now block builds)
- Prettier-formatted entire codebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(v2): full site overhaul — pages, design system, SEO infra

PAGES
- /         home with Hero, Arc, Now, Research, Work, Projects, Knowledge, Writing
- /about    Life Changelog timeline (17 real milestones)
- /research 4 papers with ScholarlyArticle JSON-LD + ML stack + PyTorch issue
- /experience 8 reverse-chrono work entries (Benmore, Harvard, ZeroSync, BCH, BU, Battery)
- /projects 9 projects (Cattle Logic, SpatialDINO, Foundry CLI, Flink, etc.)
- /work     4 internal tools/CLIs (Foundry, RTK, Skills, Excalidraw)
- /writing  + [slug] index with tag filters, post page with Article JSON-LD
- /knowledge + [domain] 6-domain hub (AI, Finance, Distributed Systems, Math, Physics, Software)
- /404      branded not-found
- /VC, /Volunteering → redirect to /experience and /about

DESIGN SYSTEM
- Tailwind theme: bg, surface, border, primary teal, accent neon cyan, muted
- UI: Button, Card (glow), Badge, StatBadge
- Sections: Hero, SectionHeader, PaperCard, ProjectCard, ExperienceCard, TimelineItem

SEO
- JsonLd component with Person/Article/ScholarlyArticle schemas
- Meta with OG + Twitter cards + canonical URLs + <link rel="me">
- pages/sitemap.xml.js server-rendered sitemap with priority + changefreq
- pages/robots.txt.js with sitemap pointer
- Person JSON-LD on every page via Layout

LAYOUT
- Sticky Nav with active-route highlight + 7 links
- Footer with site map, social, /architecture link
- Dark theme everywhere; custom scrollbar; smooth scrolling

DATA
- lib/data.js: PAPERS, EXPERIENCE, PROJECTS, WORK_TOOLS, TIMELINE (17), KNOWLEDGE_DOMAINS, WRITING
- lib/site.js: SITE constants, NAV_LINKS
- Bio updated: junior at BU → Head of FDE at Benmore, Harvard Kirchhausen, 4 papers

Build passes (15 routes), lint clean (2 pre-existing legacy warnings).
CHANGELOG updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(v2): finance Thesis Tracker + Trade Log, SVG favicon, screenshot ignore

- /knowledge/finance now renders ThesisTracker (5 theses) + TradeLog (4 trades)
- New components: ThesisTracker, TradeLog
- New data module: lib/finance.js (THESES, TRADES)
- SVG favicon (teal "a" on dark slate) wired through Meta
- gitignore root-level *.png screenshots
- All 11 routes verified live; CHANGELOG updated
